fact_0001.statement: Retinoids regulate gene transcription by binding to RAR and RXR nuclear receptors.

fact_0004.statement: Topical retinoids increase procollagen production to augment collagen formation in the dermis.

fact_0005.statement: Tretinoin is the gold standard for clinical improvement of photoaged skin.

fact_0007.statement: Adapalene selectively binds to retinoic acid receptors RAR-beta and RAR-gamma.

fact_0009.statement: Tazarotene selectively binds to nuclear retinoic acid receptors RAR-beta and RAR-gamma.

fact_0010.statement: Tazarotene 0.1% cream is effective for treating facial photodamage, including fine wrinkles and hyperpigmentation.

fact_0011.statement: Retinaldehyde requires only a single metabolic conversion step to active retinoic acid in keratinocytes.

fact_0014.statement: Retinyl palmitate nano-formulation reduces inflammatory and non-inflammatory lesions in mild acne.

fact_0015.statement: Topical retinoids are contraindicated during pregnancy due to potential teratogenic risks.

fact_0017.statement: Patient-reported significant improvement in acne in over 90% of patients using a glycolic+salicylic serum (uncontrolled questionnaire study, n=66).

fact_0018.statement: salicylic-mandelic acid peels are effective for active acne lesions and post-acne hyperpigmentation

fact_0019.statement: topical 30% salicylic acid peels reduce fine lines, hyperpigmentation, and skin roughness in photoaged skin

fact_0020.statement: glycolic acid peels clinical application improves mild facial photoaging

fact_0021.statement: topical l-ascorbic acid promotes collagen synthesis and protects against ultraviolet-induced photodamage

fact_0022.statement: topical 5% sodium ascorbyl phosphate lotion is effective for acne vulgaris management

fact_0024.statement: topical niacinamide enhances ceramide synthesis and improves skin barrier function

fact_0025.statement: topical niacinamide reduces inflammatory lesions and regulates sebum excretion in acne vulgaris

fact_0028.statement: topical L-ascorbic acid formulation requires a pH of less than 3.5 to achieve percutaneous absorption

fact_0029.statement: maximal percutaneous absorption of topical L-ascorbic acid occurs at a concentration of 20%

fact_0030.statement: topical L-ascorbic acid increases mRNA levels of collagen types I and iii in human skin in vivo

fact_0034.statement: topical niacinamide reduces hyperpigmentation by suppressing melanosome transfer from melanocytes to keratinocytes

fact_0035.statement: topical niacinamide 4% is effective in treating melasma with comparable efficacy to 4% hydroquinone but with fewer side effects

fact_0036.statement: topical niacinamide 2% and 5% concentrations significantly decrease cutaneous hyperpigmentation and increase skin lightness

fact_0038.statement: topical niacinamide improves stratum corneum barrier function and provides clinical benefits in subjects with rosacea

fact_0039.statement: topical niacinamide reduces transepidermal water loss and increases epidermal stratum corneum hydration

fact_0041.statement: 45% mandelic acid peels are equally effective as 30% salicylic acid peels for mild-to-moderate acne vulgaris with better safety profile

fact_0044.statement: topical L-ascorbic acid formulation requires a pH of less than 3.5 to achieve percutaneous absorption

fact_0045.statement: maximal percutaneous absorption of topical L-ascorbic acid occurs at a concentration of 20%

fact_0046.statement: topical L-ascorbic acid increases mRNA levels of collagen types I and iii in human skin in vivo

fact_0047.statement: topical 5% niacinamide reduces fine lines, wrinkles, hyperpigmented spots, texture, red blotchiness, and yellowing in aging facial skin

fact_0048.statement: topical alpha-hydroxy acids application to photoaged skin results in increased skin thickness, improved elastic fibers, and increased collagen density

fact_0049.statement: salicylic acid acts as a desmolytic agent by disrupting cellular junctions to exfoliate the stratum corneum and possesses comedolytic properties useful for acne

fact_0050.statement: topical 20% salicylic-10% mandelic acid combination peel is effective for the treatment of active acne and postacne hyperpigmentation in Asian skin

fact_0051.statement: topical palmitoyl pentapeptide-4 (pal-KTTKS) stimulates collagen production, provides significant improvement in fine lines and wrinkles, and is safe and well-tolerated

fact_0052.statement: topical copper tripeptide-1 (GHK-Cu) stimulates collagen and glycosaminoglycan synthesis during tissue remodeling

fact_0053.statement: topical GHK-Cu peptide regulates gene expression related to skin regeneration, collagen synthesis, and antioxidant protection

fact_0054.statement: topical acetyl hexapeptide-8 (Argireline) mimics SNAP-25 and destabilizes the SNARE complex to inhibit neurotransmitter release and reduce wrinkle depth

fact_0057.statement: topical palmitoyl tripeptide-38 formulation improves skin roughness, tone, and reduces wrinkles in aged facial skin

fact_0058.statement: topical acetyl tetrapeptide-5 has protective anti-aging effects on skin fibroblasts, acts as an angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitor, and is safe and well-tolerated

fact_0063.statement: 15% azelaic acid gel effectively improved acne-induced post-inflammatory erythema and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation with minimal adverse reactions

fact_0064.statement: Azelaic acid is more effective than vehicle for rosacea, acne and melasma

fact_0065.statement: In a systematic review of topical treatments for post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation, retinoids and hydroxy acids were supported by the greatest number of high-quality studies, with broad-spectrum sunscreen recommended

fact_0066.statement: Niacinamide is an effective skin lightening compound that works by inhibiting melanosome transfer from melanocytes to keratinocytes

fact_0067.statement: A systematic review of randomized controlled trials found microneedling is an effective and well-tolerated treatment for atrophic acne scars with no serious adverse effects reported

fact_0068.statement: Combined microneedling and chemical peeling is superior to either treatment alone for atrophic acne scars, with favourable safety and patient-reported outcomes

fact_0070.statement: Meta-analysis found fractional picosecond laser achieves clinical improvement of atrophic acne scars similar to other fractional lasers but with lower risk of post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation and lower pain

fact_0071.statement: Long-term treatment with adapalene 0.3 percent and benzoyl peroxide 2.5 percent gel reduces atrophic acne scar count, supporting early effective acne treatment to prevent and reduce scarring

fact_0072.statement: A 70% glycolic acid peel is an effective alternative to trichloroacetic acid peel for treating facial atrophic acne scars, especially for patients not tolerating TCA or requiring lesser downtime

fact_0073.statement: The chemical reconstruction of skin scars (CROSS) technique using trichloroacetic acid is well-tolerated and effective for treating ice pick atrophic acne scars

fact_0074.statement: Azelaic acid's inhibitory effect on melanocytes makes it widely used in the treatment of hyperpigmentation disorders such as melasma and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation

fact_0075.statement: Subcision is a surgical technique for managing atrophic acne scars.

fact_0076.statement: Subcision is a safe and effective method for treating atrophic acne scars.

fact_0077.statement: Subcision is the most commonly used treatment technique for rolling atrophic acne scars.

fact_0078.statement: Ablative fractional carbon dioxide laser is an effective therapy for the treatment of atrophic acne scars.

fact_0079.statement: Fractional radiofrequency is superior for treating atrophic acne scar patients prone to pigmentation.

fact_0080.statement: Oral tranexamic acid produces the greatest reduction in MASI scores among administration routes for melasma in a meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.

fact_0081.statement: A network meta-analysis estimated the optimal oral tranexamic acid dose for melasma as 250 mg three times daily for 12 weeks.

fact_0082.statement: Kojic acid is a natural fungal metabolite that inhibits tyrosinase activity in the synthesis of melanin.

fact_0083.statement: Thiamidol (isobutylamido thiazolyl resorcinol) is the most potent inhibitor of human tyrosinase among the resorcinyl-thiazole derivatives screened.

fact_0084.statement: Many cosmeceutical agents for facial hyperpigmentation are thought to work through inhibition of tyrosinase, a key enzyme of melanogenesis.

fact_0085.statement: A non-fractional 1540 nm erbium:glass laser is safe and effective in the treatment of atrophic acne scars.

fact_0086.statement: Platelet-rich plasma appears to be a safe and effective treatment for various types of atrophic scars.

fact_0087.statement: Combining microneedling with platelet-rich plasma is more effective and safe with fewer sessions than either alone for atrophic scars.

fact_0088.statement: Evidence-based review found stronger support for lasers, platelet-rich plasma, chemical peels, hyaluronic acid fillers and microneedling, and lesser-quality evidence for microdermabrasion, subcision and lipoaspirate grafting in atrophic acne scars.

fact_0089.statement: Dual-plane hyaluronic acid filler treatment leads to improvement in the depth of atrophic acne scars in only two sessions.

fact_0090.statement: Adapalene 0.1% gel is significantly less irritating than tretinoin 0.025% gel when tested in acne patients.

fact_0091.statement: A significantly greater number of acne patients preferred adapalene gel 0.1% to tretinoin microsphere gel 0.1% (72.5% vs 27.5%).

fact_0092.statement: Irritant reactions are more prominent with tretinoin and tazarotene, whereas retinaldehyde and retinol are considerably less irritating.

fact_0093.statement: Most topical anti-aging comparators were found to be less irritating and better tolerated by patients than tretinoin in a systematic review.

fact_0094.statement: Retinol, retinyl esters and retinaldehyde used in many cosmeceutical products are efficacious, safe and well-tolerated.
